# T1030 — Data Transfer Size Limits

**Tactics:** Exfiltration  ·  **Platforms:** Linux, macOS, Windows, ESXi

## Description

An adversary may exfiltrate data in fixed size chunks instead of whole files or limit packet sizes below certain thresholds. This approach may be used to avoid triggering network data transfer threshold alerts.

## Detection

**Detection Strategy for Data Transfer Size Limits and Chunked Exfiltration**
- Adversary uses a process to establish outbound connections that transmit uniform packet sizes at a consistent interval, avoiding threshold-based network alerts. _(log sources: NSM:Flow, WinEventLog:Sysmon)_
- Outbound connections from non-network-facing processes repeatedly send similarly sized payloads within uniform time intervals. _(log sources: NSM:Flow, auditd:SYSCALL)_
- Processes on macOS initiate external connections that consistently transmit data in fixed sizes using LaunchAgents or unexpected users. _(log sources: macos:endpointsecurity, macos:unifiedlog)_

## Mitigations

- **Network Intrusion Prevention** (M1031): Use intrusion detection signatures to block traffic at network boundaries.

## Reference

- ATT&CK: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1030
